summary: In 2026 LinkedIn ranks the feed with a unified AI/embeddings foundation model publicly discussed as 360Brew (a ~150B-parameter decoder-only model trained on LinkedIn's own profiles, posts and interactions). The system moved from a relationship graph to an interest graph and from optimizing viral reach to optimizing depth, relevance and topical authority: it semantically cross-references each post against the author's profile (Headline/About/Experience) and suppresses distribution when there is dissonance (e.g. a "graphic designer" posting crypto advice). Ranking runs in stages — a quality/spam filter, an LLM retrieval pass that narrows millions of candidates to ~2,000 via embeddings + cosine similarity, a generative recommender that re-ranks using ~1,000+ of the viewer's recent interactions, then a small early-engagement test audience that gates broader distribution. Engagement signals are weighted by depth: dwell time and comment quality outrank likes (comments worth roughly 15x a like, with 30-60+ second dwell as the key threshold), and the first 60-90 minute "golden hour" decides a post's fate. Reach has structurally reset downward — Richard van der Blom's Algorithm Insights 2026 (1.3M posts / 50k creators) reports views down ~50%, engagement down ~25%, follower growth down ~59% YoY, and reach down ~60% for active creators over two years — while penalties hit external links (~50-60% less reach), engagement bait, pods, edited posts, frequent identical reposts and unedited AI content. Personal profiles dominate distribution (~65% of feed allocation, 4-5x the engagement) over company pages (~5% allocation, organic reach down 60-66% since 2024).

guidelines.guideline: Treat the feed as ranked by an embeddings-based AI foundation model (360Brew), not a likes counter — optimize for semantic relevance and predicted dwell, not raw reactions
guidelines.how: Write tightly on a consistent topic so your member embedding and content embedding align; the model retrieves ~2,000 candidate posts via cosine similarity then re-ranks against the viewer's ~1,000 recent interactions
guidelines.why: 360Brew is a ~150B-parameter decoder-only model trained on LinkedIn data that reasons about intent/relevance probabilistically; off-topic posts read as low-confidence noise and get throttled

guidelines.guideline: Optimize for the interest graph, not your connection network — content is shown to people who engage with your TOPIC, even if they don't know you
guidelines.how: Use clear topical signals (consistent subject matter, vocabulary, entities) so the model can match your post to interested non-followers; stop relying on 'my network will see it'
guidelines.why: LinkedIn moved from a relationship graph to an interest graph in 2025-2026; follower count and reach are now structurally decoupled (8k focused followers can beat 80k unfocused)

guidelines.guideline: Make your Headline, About and Experience explicitly state the expertise you post about — the algorithm semantically cross-references post vs profile before distributing
guidelines.how: Align profile keywords with content pillars; if you post about GEO/AI marketing, your profile must say so; avoid topic-hopping that dilutes the semantic signal
guidelines.why: 360Brew detects dissonance between stated expertise and post topic and suppresses distribution; reinforcing stated expertise unlocks broader reach. It takes ~90 days of consistent topical posting for the model to categorize your expertise

guidelines.guideline: Engineer the first 60-90 minutes deliberately — the post passes a quality/spam classifier, then a small test audience (~2-5% of network), and only advances if early engagement clears the bar
guidelines.how: Stage 1 (0-60 min, shown to ~2-5%) needs ~5%+ engagement to advance; below ~2% kills the post. Stage 2 (1-6h) expands to 10-20% + 2nd degree; Stage 3 (6h+, <1% of posts) reaches outside network
guidelines.why: Distribution is gated stage-by-stage by early-engagement quality; failing the golden-hour test caps total reach permanently for that post

guidelines.guideline: Be present and drive substantive engagement in the first 60-90 minutes (the 'golden hour')
guidelines.how: Post at peak audience times, reply to every comment fast, and aim for 3+ meaningful comments in the first 60 minutes (≈5.2x reach amplification). Notify a few genuinely relevant people — not a pod
guidelines.why: Early engagement velocity, especially comments within the first hour, is the strongest gating signal for whether a post advances to wider distribution

guidelines.guideline: Prioritize comments above all other engagement — a comment is worth roughly 15x a like
guidelines.how: Write posts that invite genuine discussion (a real question, a contrarian take, an open prompt), and reply with substance to keep comment threads alive 24-48h
guidelines.why: Comment quality is a top-tier signal in 360Brew; the depth score accumulates over 24-48h from sustained commenting and can expand or contract distribution

guidelines.guideline: Optimize for dwell time (time-on-post) — aim to hold readers 30-60+ seconds
guidelines.how: Use story structure, strong hooks, line breaks, and 'see more' cliffhangers; carousels/documents pull 3-5 min dwell; native video gives 2-3x text dwell. 150-300 word text posts read best
guidelines.why: Dwell time is the single highest-weighted signal; 0-3s dwell ≈1.2% engagement vs 61s+ dwell ≈15.6% engagement (a ~13x difference)

guidelines.guideline: Create save-worthy and share-worthy content (frameworks, checklists, how-tos) — saves and shares are stronger quality signals than likes
guidelines.how: Build 'reference' value: numbered frameworks, cheat-sheets, carousels people return to; a save signals lasting value, a share signals endorsement
guidelines.why: Saves and shares rank above likes in the 2026 weighting; they indicate durable value, which the model rewards with extended distribution

guidelines.guideline: Set expectations against the new lower baseline — do not benchmark against 2023-2024 numbers
guidelines.how: Plan for ~50% lower views, ~25% lower engagement, ~59% lower follower growth YoY; measure relative performance (your best vs your average), not absolute reach
guidelines.why: van der Blom Algorithm Insights 2026 (1.3M posts, 50k creators): the average reach has permanently reset to a lower baseline; reach is down ~60% for active creators over two years

guidelines.guideline: Keep external links OUT of the post body — put the link in the first comment or omit it
guidelines.how: Native-first content; if a link is essential, drop it in the first comment (now ~10-15% reduction vs 5-10% before) or edit it in after the golden hour; one body link cuts median reach ~18.8% to 50-70% depending on source
guidelines.why: The algorithm treats a body link as an 'exit sign' that keeps users off-platform; body URLs cost roughly 50-60% (range 45-70%) of organic reach

guidelines.guideline: Never use engagement bait ('Comment YES if you agree', 'tag 3 people', reaction polls as bait)
guidelines.how: Replace bait with genuine open questions tied to the topic; ask for opinions/experiences, not mechanical reactions
guidelines.why: 2026 NLP classifiers detect and suppress engagement bait by up to ~60%; the authenticity-first shift actively rewards real conversation over manufactured reactions

guidelines.guideline: Avoid engagement pods and reciprocal-engagement rings entirely
guidelines.how: Build real topical communities instead; if you notify people, make it relevant and non-reciprocal
guidelines.why: AI detects reciprocal engagement patterns with ~97% accuracy and applies silent shadowbans / reach penalties up to ~45% (GrowWithGhost 2026)

guidelines.guideline: Do not publish unedited, generic AI output — every post must carry a specific personal insight and voice
guidelines.how: Avoid templated openers ('In today's fast-paced world'), bullet-heavy no-voice structure, repeated frameworks and low sentence variation; inject first-hand examples, numbers, opinions
guidelines.why: NLP classifiers flag unedited AI patterns and sharply cut initial distribution; generic AI also produces near-zero dwell, no saves and no discussion, which kills ranking organically

guidelines.guideline: Avoid editing posts during the golden hour and avoid frequent identical reposts
guidelines.how: Finalize copy before publishing (edits during early ranking can disrupt distribution); space out reposts and add genuinely new value rather than recycling identical content; instant reposts of others outperform 'repost with thought' by a wide margin for the original author's signal
guidelines.why: Editing mid-test can reset/disrupt the early-engagement evaluation; mass identical reposting is flagged as low-value recycling and throttled

guidelines.guideline: Build for a long tail — strong posts now live 2-3 weeks, not 24-48 hours, IF they keep earning comments and dwell
guidelines.how: Keep replying to comments for days; resurface the thread; design evergreen, reference-grade content that keeps accumulating depth score
guidelines.why: In 2026 high-performing content stays active in feeds for 2-3 weeks as long as it keeps generating comments and time-spent; the depth score compounds over 24-48h and beyond

guidelines.guideline: Favor document carousels and native vertical video — the two highest-distribution formats in 2026
guidelines.how: Document/PDF carousels hit ~6.6% engagement (highest of any format, 3-5 min dwell); native vertical video 15-90s (portrait) is getting unmatched non-follower distribution; text-only struggles to break ~2-4%
guidelines.why: Carousels maximize dwell and saves; native short vertical video is the single biggest new distribution opportunity in the 2026 algorithm and is being pushed to non-followers

guidelines.guideline: Use 0-3 hashtags as default — extra hashtags no longer help and can slightly hurt
guidelines.how: Drop generic hashtag stacks; if used, keep to 0-3 topically precise tags; rely on semantic topic signals instead
guidelines.why: Posts without hashtags now outperform posts with hashtags by ~5-10%; the interest graph reads topic from content semantics, not tags

guidelines.guideline: Distribute through personal profiles (employee/founder voices), not the company page, for organic reach
guidelines.how: Run an employee-advocacy / founder-led model; reserve the company page for evergreen brand reference, ads and retargeting audiences, not organic reach goals
guidelines.why: Personal profiles get ~65% of feed allocation and ~4.7% engagement vs company pages at ~5% allocation and ~1-2% engagement (5-10x gap); company-page organic reach fell ~60-66% from 2024 to early 2026

guidelines.guideline: If you must post from a company page, lead with people, native video and employee amplification
guidelines.how: Post native video and carousels, tag and feature employees, and have staff personal profiles engage in the golden hour to seed distribution; avoid links and salesy CTAs
guidelines.why: Brand pages get fewer peer comments, so they structurally underperform the golden-hour comment threshold; employee engagement is the main lever to recover any reach

guidelines.guideline: Post consistently but cap volume — quality and topical focus beat frequency
guidelines.how: Stay within ~1 post/day (hard ceiling ~5-7/day triggers spam signals); maintain a consistent topical cadence over ~90 days to build embedding authority
guidelines.why: Rapid high-volume posting is a negative signal; consistency on-topic is what trains 360Brew to recognize and reward your expertise

summary: In 2026 a LinkedIn personal profile is the single most decisive surface for both human recruiters and AI-driven matching, with verified, complete profiles seeing roughly 60% more views (verification) and 21x more views / 36x more messages (photo) than incomplete ones. The architecture is unchanged on core limits — headline 220 chars, About 2,600 chars, banner 1584x396 (4:1), photo 400x400 (1:1) — but two structural shifts define the year: Creator Mode's on/off toggle was retired (March 2024), so creator tools (Follow-primary, newsletter, enhanced analytics, hashtag/topic display) are now accessed individually by all members; and free identity verification (CLEAR in US/CA/MX, Persona via NFC passport in 60+ countries as of April 2026) plus workplace/education verification has become a baseline trust signal. The Social Selling Index (SSI, 0-100, four 25-point pillars, 90-day rolling window) remains the canonical self-improvement metric, with profile completeness the fastest lever. Best practice has moved from keyword-stuffing to a value-formula headline, a hook-first About (first ~265 chars desktop / ~200 mobile visible before "see more"), a curated 3-piece Featured section, pinned Top 3 Skills mapped to recruiter search, and consistent posting (even 1x/week) to feed both SSI and follower growth.

guidelines.guideline: Upload a square headshot at 400x400px (min 300x300, upload 640x640 for retina sharpness), 1:1 ratio, JPG/PNG, max 8MB
guidelines.how: Face fills ~60% of frame, eyes on upper third, neutral/branded background, sRGB color, 85-95% JPEG quality; design for the CIRCULAR crop (corners are cut)
guidelines.why: Profiles with a photo get ~21x more views and ~36x more messages; the circular crop means corner content is lost

guidelines.guideline: Use a 1584x396px banner (4:1 aspect ratio), JPG/PNG/GIF, max 8MB
guidelines.how: Keep text/logo in the right two-thirds; the circular photo overlaps the bottom-LEFT (~200px from left, ~150px from bottom on desktop). Safe zone: 300px from left, 150px from right, 60px top, 80px bottom
guidelines.why: The banner is prime free advertising real estate; left content is obscured by the avatar and right content is cropped on mobile

guidelines.guideline: Use your real first/last name only (no titles/emojis in name fields); add a name-pronunciation audio clip
guidelines.how: Record the pronunciation clip in the MOBILE app only (10 seconds, ~25 words max), tap speaker icon; restrict to 1st-degree connections via audience selector if desired
guidelines.why: Name fields are matched in search; stuffing them risks restrictions. Pronunciation builds rapport and inclusivity

guidelines.guideline: Max 220 characters — never leave it as the auto-filled job title
guidelines.how: Use a value formula: [Role/Outcome] + [Who you help] + [How/Proof] + [keywords]. Front-load the most important keyword (only ~first 40-70 chars show in feed/search/comments). Use | or • as separators
guidelines.why: The headline is the most-seen field (search, feed, comments, connection requests) and is heavily weighted by both recruiter search and SSI 'professional brand' pillar

guidelines.guideline: Max 2,600 characters; the first ~265 chars (desktop) / ~200 chars (mobile, ~3 lines) are visible before 'see more'
guidelines.how: Open with a hook (bold claim, problem statement, surprising stat, or value prop) in the first 2-3 lines. Write in first person, include target keywords naturally, use short paragraphs/line breaks, end with a CTA and contact path
guidelines.why: Truncation means the hook before 'see more' decides whether anyone expands; keyword density here feeds recruiter and AI matching

guidelines.guideline: Claim a custom vanity URL (slug 3-100 chars, letters/numbers/hyphens, no spaces/special chars)
guidelines.how: Edit via the 'Edit public profile & URL' panel (top-right of profile). Changeable up to 5 times in 180 days; old URL keeps working ~180 days unless unlinked. Use firstname-lastname format
guidelines.why: A clean URL is shareable on CVs/email signatures, looks credible, and is more index-friendly

guidelines.guideline: Set a specific location (city/metro) and the most relevant industry
guidelines.how: Use the metro area where you want opportunities (recruiters filter by location); pick the industry closest to your target market, not your literal employer's sector
guidelines.why: Both are hard filters in recruiter and Sales Navigator search; a generic or wrong value removes you from results

guidelines.guideline: Fill contact info: website(s), email, and other links; add up to the allowed website/link slots
guidelines.how: Use custom link anchor text (not just 'Company Website'); add portfolio, newsletter, booking link; keep email professional. Link Featured items rather than burying URLs in About
guidelines.why: Contact completeness improves discoverability and conversion; links drive traffic off-platform

guidelines.guideline: Curate exactly 3 strong items (posts, articles, links, media) rather than many weak ones
guidelines.how: Pin your best-performing post, a lead-magnet/portfolio link, and a credibility piece (press, case study). Use custom thumbnails; refresh quarterly
guidelines.why: 3 strong pieces outperform 12 mediocre ones; Featured is high-visibility and contributes to the SSI 'professional brand' pillar

guidelines.guideline: Job title max 100 chars; write rich descriptions with metrics; attach media
guidelines.how: Use 3-5 achievement bullets per role with quantified results (%, $, #), seed keywords, and attach media/links/documents. Keep current role's description longest
guidelines.why: Experience descriptions are searched and feed AI summaries; media attachments add proof and dwell time

guidelines.guideline: Add up to 50 skills (LinkedIn's hard cap); pin the Top 3 (formerly limited; some sources cite 100 cap, but the enforced/displayed list and recruiter-weighted ceiling is 50 with 3 pinned)
guidelines.how: Map skills to exact recruiter search terms; pin the 3 most strategic to 'Top Skills'. Take Skill Assessments to earn 'Verified' badges on your top 3-5
guidelines.why: Skills are a primary recruiter search field; verified/endorsed skills rank higher and can yield up to ~30% more recruiter messages; 99+ endorsements signals strong social proof (display caps at 99)

guidelines.guideline: Drive endorsements on pinned Top 3 skills; aim for 99+ on key skills
guidelines.how: Endorse peers to prompt reciprocity; ask connections directly for endorsements on specific strategic skills; remove off-target endorsements that dilute relevance
guidelines.why: Endorsed profiles are far more likely to surface in recruiter search; concentration on relevant skills beats scattered endorsements

guidelines.guideline: Actively request written recommendations from managers, clients, and peers
guidelines.how: Request via the profile (give/request flow); ask for specific, outcome-focused text; offer to write one first; target a steady cadence rather than a one-time batch
guidelines.why: Recommendations are strong third-party trust signals and contribute to the SSI 'professional brand' pillar and recruiter confidence

guidelines.guideline: Use 'Open to Work' (green frame public OR recruiters-only), 'Providing Services', and 'Open to Hiring' as fits your goal
guidelines.how: Set 'Open to Work' to 'recruiters only' if employed to stay discreet; 'Providing Services' creates a free service-listing page surfaced in service search; 'Open to Hiring' adds a #Hiring frame
guidelines.why: These flags inject you into dedicated LinkedIn search/match flows (recruiter pools, services marketplace, hiring discovery)

guidelines.guideline: Turn on 'Make follow primary' so the Follow button leads; Creator Mode toggle was retired (Mar 2024) — tools are now set individually
guidelines.how: Settings & Privacy > Visibility > Followers > toggle 'Make follow primary'. Set up newsletter, display selected topics/hashtags, and access enhanced analytics directly
guidelines.why: Follow-primary grows an audience beyond the connection cap and unlocks the creator surface; tools no longer require a single on/off switch

guidelines.guideline: Launch a LinkedIn newsletter once eligible (>150 followers/connections + history of original content)
guidelines.how: Create from the post composer ('Create a newsletter'); publish on a fixed cadence; subscribers get email + notification each issue; track via newsletter analytics (impressions, article views, email sends, email open rate)
guidelines.why: Newsletters auto-notify all subscribers per issue (a distribution multiplier) and are favored in the 2026 algorithm for long-form value

guidelines.guideline: Track SSI at linkedin.com/sales/ssi (0-100, four 25-point pillars, 90-day rolling, updates daily)
guidelines.how: Pillar 1 Establish brand: complete profile 100%, photo/banner/headline/About/Featured/recommendations (fastest 5-8 pt win). Pillar 2 Find right people: use search/filters, keep connection ACCEPTANCE rate high (avoid spray-and-pray). Pillar 3 Engage with insights: post and comment consistently with relevant content. Pillar 4 Build relationships: connect with senior/relevant people and nurture them
guidelines.why: Industry-average SSI is ~35; 75+ signals thought-leader status; it is the canonical, daily-updating self-optimization metric

guidelines.guideline: Add profile content in additional languages (secondary locales) when targeting multiple markets
guidelines.how: Use 'Add profile in another language' to create a fully translated version (name, headline, About, experience) shown to viewers in that locale
guidelines.why: Multilingual profiles surface correctly to region-specific searchers and AI matching

guidelines.guideline: Set public profile visibility to maximize discoverability while controlling sensitive fields
guidelines.how: Settings > Visibility > 'Edit your public profile' — enable public visibility, choose which sections show to logged-out/search-engine viewers; control 'Profile viewing options' and activity broadcasts
guidelines.why: Public visibility is what lets search engines and non-members find you; over-restriction hides you from inbound opportunity

guidelines.guideline: Get the free verified badge: identity (CLEAR in US/CA/MX, Persona via NFC passport in 60+ countries as of Apr 2026), workplace (work email or Microsoft Entra), and education
guidelines.how: Profile > 'Verifications'. CLEAR: phone/email + selfie + government ID. Persona: NFC passport scan + selfie. Workplace: verify via work email or Entra Verified ID
guidelines.why: Verified members average ~60% more profile views; the badge is a rising baseline trust signal against fake/AI profiles

guidelines.guideline: Monitor post and creator analytics (impressions, members reached, engagement, follower growth, demographics)
guidelines.how: Access via the analytics dashboard on profile and per-post 'View analytics'; review audience demographics to refine topics; use newsletter analytics for email open rate
guidelines.why: Data-driven iteration on content feeds the SSI 'engage with insights' pillar and compounds reach and follower growth

pitfalls: Leaving the headline as the auto-filled job title — wastes the single most-seen field and the strongest SSI/recruiter keyword slot
Writing an About section with a weak first 2-3 lines — everything after ~200-265 chars is hidden behind 'see more' and most readers never expand
Putting logo/text on the LEFT of the banner where the circular avatar overlaps it, or near right/mobile crop zones
Designing the photo without accounting for the circular crop — corners get cut
Trying to 'turn on Creator Mode' — the toggle was retired in March 2024; tools are now enabled individually (Follow-primary, newsletter, analytics)
Spraying connection requests with a low acceptance rate — this actively LOWERS the SSI 'find the right people' pillar
Maxing out skills with 40-50 generic terms instead of pinning 3 and concentrating endorsements on strategic, recruiter-searched skills
Skipping free identity/workplace verification — leaving a ~60% view uplift and a key anti-fraud trust signal on the table
Over-restricting public visibility so search engines and non-members can't find the profile
Not claiming a custom vanity URL — leaving an ugly numeric URL on CVs and email signatures
Relying on removed features (Cover Story 30s hover video and native carousels were retired Dec 14, 2023) in profile strategy
Stuffing the name fields with titles/emojis/keywords — risks account restriction and breaks search matching

summary: In 2026 the LinkedIn Company Page (now "LinkedIn Page") is built from a fixed set of fields with hard limits: a 120-character tagline, a 2,000-character About/overview, up to 20 specialties, one industry plus optional company type, a company-size band, headquarters plus additional locations, a website, up to 3 community hashtags, and a custom CTA button. Branding assets are precise: logo recommended 400x400px (min 268x268px, displayed at 300x300) and cover image 4200x700px (rendered ~1128x191), both PNG/JPEG up to 3MB. The page is governed by a four-tier admin model (Super Admin, Content Admin, Curator, Analyst) plus separate paid-media roles (Sponsored Content Poster, Lead Gen Forms Manager, Landing Pages Manager, plus Recruiting/recruiter roles via Recruiter), where a person can hold one page role and multiple paid-media roles at once. Free Page verification (work email or Microsoft Entra Verified ID, completed in the mobile app) now grants a verified badge, and the page can spawn up to 25 Showcase Pages, Product Pages (one category each, 5 media files, 6 CTA options, reviews/ratings), a Services page (≤500 employees, up to 10 services), and Events. SEO matters: the public/vanity URL, keyword-rich About first lines, specialties and a complete profile drive both LinkedIn internal search and Google indexing of the page.

guidelines.guideline: Use the exact legal/brand name as the Page name and claim a clean vanity (public) URL in the form linkedin.com/company/yourcompanyname
guidelines.how: Set the name when creating the Page (linkedin.com/company/setup/new); edit the public URL under Admin tools > Edit public URL. Keep it short, no numbers/hyphens, matching the brand exactly
guidelines.why: The vanity URL is indexed by Google and is the canonical identifier; a clean name+URL improves brand recognition and external SEO

guidelines.guideline: Upload a square logo at 400x400px (LinkedIn's recommended size; absolute minimum 268x268px), PNG or JPEG, under 3MB, displayed on-page at 300x300
guidelines.how: Admin view > Edit page > Overview > Logo. Use a transparent or solid-fill PNG that reads on both light and dark backgrounds
guidelines.why: The logo renders in feed, search and employee profiles; correct dimensions prevent blur/cropping and 1:1 ratio is enforced

guidelines.guideline: Upload a cover/banner at 4200x700px (LinkedIn's recommended and minimum), PNG/JPEG, max 3MB; the page renders it at roughly 1128x191 (about 6:1)
guidelines.how: Edit page > Overview > Cover image. Keep logos/text centered because mobile crops toward the center (~900px wide)
guidelines.why: Edges are cropped across screen sizes; centering load-bearing content avoids losing key text/branding on mobile

guidelines.guideline: Write a tagline of 120 characters or fewer that states the core value proposition / elevator pitch
guidelines.how: Edit page > Tagline. Lead with what you do and for whom; include one primary keyword
guidelines.why: The tagline shows directly under the name in search results and the top card; it is the first descriptor a visitor and the algorithm read

guidelines.guideline: Use up to 2,000 characters; front-load the most important keywords and value in the first ~1-2 lines because that is what previews and search surface
guidelines.how: Edit page > Overview > Description. Structure: who you serve, what you offer, proof, and a clear CTA. Repeat target keywords naturally (industry, services, location)
guidelines.why: The About text feeds LinkedIn internal search and is indexed by Google; the opening lines are truncated in previews so keyword placement early maximizes discovery

guidelines.guideline: Add up to 20 specialties as discrete keyword tags covering services, technologies, audiences and locations
guidelines.how: Edit page > Overview > Specialties. Use the actual search terms prospects type, not internal jargon
guidelines.why: Specialties are a dedicated keyword field that strongly influences LinkedIn search matching and content relevance

guidelines.guideline: Select the single most accurate industry from LinkedIn's predefined list (one industry per page)
guidelines.how: Edit page > Overview > Industry
guidelines.why: Industry drives filtering, recommendations and competitive benchmarking; it cannot be free-text

guidelines.guideline: Set company type (Public company, Privately held, Nonprofit, Government agency, Sole proprietorship, Self-employed, Partnership) and the size band (e.g. 1-10, 11-50, 51-200, 201-500, 501-1000, 1001-5000, 5001-10000, 10001+; Self-employed = 1)
guidelines.how: Edit page > Overview > Company size / type
guidelines.why: Size band gates feature eligibility (e.g. Services page requires <=500 employees) and personalizes how LinkedIn categorizes the org

guidelines.guideline: Add a headquarters location plus all additional office locations
guidelines.how: Edit page > Locations > Add location; mark one as HQ
guidelines.why: Locations improve local/geographic search relevance and appear on the page; multiple locations support multi-market discovery

guidelines.guideline: Add the primary website URL with full https://
guidelines.how: Edit page > Overview > Website
guidelines.why: Drives referral traffic and is a trust/SEO signal; also a destination option for the CTA button

guidelines.guideline: Associate up to 3 community hashtags to engage as the Page in those feeds
guidelines.how: From the Page admin view, use the hashtags module (Manage hashtags) to add up to three
guidelines.why: Lets the page comment/react under its own identity in topic feeds, expanding reach beyond followers

guidelines.guideline: Enable a custom button and pick the action that matches the goal (options include Visit website, Contact us, Learn more, Register, Sign up; Product Pages offer Download now, Get started, Request demo, Try now, Contact us, Learn more)
guidelines.how: Edit page > Buttons > toggle Custom button > choose label and destination URL
guidelines.why: LinkedIn reports pages with a custom button see ~20% more engagement; CTA clicks and conversion rate are tracked in Analytics > Visitors

guidelines.guideline: Complete free Page verification to earn the verified badge
guidelines.how: Verify via work email or Microsoft Entra Verified ID; the verification flow runs in the LinkedIn mobile app (iOS/Android), not the web. Confirm authority to act for the company
guidelines.why: The badge raises trust and credibility; verified entities see materially higher views/engagement and reduced impersonation risk

guidelines.guideline: Limit Super Admins to a minimal trusted set (ideally 1-2); only Super Admins can add/remove any admin, edit all page info, create job posts, and deactivate the page
guidelines.how: Admin tools > Manage admins > Page admins. Assign sparingly to senior owner(s)
guidelines.why: Super Admin can delete the page and remove others; over-assignment is a security/continuity risk

guidelines.guideline: Give Content Admin to people who need to post, boost posts, and manage events/content but should not touch settings or other admins
guidelines.how: Manage admins > add as Content Admin
guidelines.why: It is the safe default for day-to-day content teams; cannot change page settings or manage users

guidelines.guideline: Use the Curator role for employees who should suggest/curate content (e.g. employee advocacy) without full posting/admin rights
guidelines.how: Manage admins > Curator
guidelines.why: Lowest-friction role for distributed content participation while limiting risk

guidelines.guideline: Assign Analyst to stakeholders who only need to view and export analytics
guidelines.how: Manage admins > Analyst
guidelines.why: Analytics-only access keeps reporting open without granting any content or settings control

guidelines.guideline: Assign paid-media roles separately from page roles: Sponsored Content Poster (create/promote ads via Campaign Manager), Lead Gen Forms Manager (download Lead Gen Form leads), Landing Pages Manager (create/edit Landing Pages); recruiting access is handled through LinkedIn Recruiter licenses
guidelines.how: Manage admins > Paid media admins tab. A person can hold a page role and multiple paid-media roles simultaneously
guidelines.why: Separation of duties: ad/lead access is decoupled from organic page control, and one person can stack paid roles without a page role

guidelines.guideline: You can grant only one page admin role per person but multiple paid-media roles; new admins generally must follow the Page and be a connection
guidelines.how: Search the member in Manage admins; if not found, have them follow the page first
guidelines.why: Prevents conflicting page permissions and ensures admins are genuine connected followers

guidelines.guideline: Spin off up to 25 Showcase Pages per parent page for distinct brands, products, business units or initiatives
guidelines.how: Admin > Create a Showcase Page; link to parent. Note daily invitation credit cap (~100 vs 250 for the main page)
guidelines.why: Lets you target sub-audiences with dedicated content and followers without diluting the main page

guidelines.guideline: Create a Product Page per product: one product category, up to 5 media files (recommend ~3 videos + 2 images, MP4), a CTA from 6 options, and member reviews/ratings with reviewer profiles
guidelines.how: Admin > Products > Add product. Add it as a skill so members can attach it to their profiles
guidelines.why: Product Pages build social proof (ratings/reviews), generate leads via the CTA, and are surfaced in product discovery

guidelines.guideline: If the company has 500 or fewer employees, add a Services page listing up to 10 services from LinkedIn's predefined categories
guidelines.how: Edit page > add Services section; pick service categories
guidelines.why: Surfaces the page in services search and enables service inquiries; gated to <=500-employee orgs

guidelines.guideline: Use LinkedIn Events from the page to host/promote webinars and in-person events, collecting registrations and attendee engagement
guidelines.how: Admin > Events > Create event; promote via posts and the page
guidelines.why: Events drive lead capture and follower growth and feed the page's content engine

guidelines.guideline: Use Page Analytics (Visitors, Followers, Content, Leads, Competitors, Search appearances, Newsletter, Custom button) to guide optimization; export for reporting
guidelines.how: Page top nav > Analytics. Custom-button CTA clicks and conversion rate live under Visitors; available to all page admins regardless of page size
guidelines.why: Demographic and CTA data inform content, audience targeting and conversion optimization

guidelines.guideline: Fully complete every field (logo, cover, tagline, about, specialties, industry, size, locations, website, hashtags, CTA)
guidelines.how: Work through Edit page until LinkedIn shows the profile as complete
guidelines.why: Complete pages get ~30% more views and rank higher in LinkedIn search; completeness is a ranking signal

summary: In 2026 LinkedIn's overall engagement rate sits around 5.2% (personal profiles ~3.85%, company pages ~2.1%), and format choice is the single biggest organic lever. The confirmed ER ordering is native document/carousel (~6.6-7.0%, +14% YoY) > multi-image (~6.8%) > native video (~5.6-5.9%) > single image (~5.0-5.2%) > text. Documents/carousels win because they generate 2-3x more dwell time than text or image posts, and the 2026 algorithm explicitly rewards dwell time, saves, comment depth and private shares over raw clicks. The external-link penalty is real and has hardened: a link in the post body cuts median reach ~18-60% (commonly cited ~50% / "halves reach"), and the old "link in first comment" workaround is now partially patched (the algorithm detects bridge behavior), leaving only a 10-15% recovery rather than full. First-60-minutes engagement and a hook within the first ~140 (mobile) / ~210 (desktop) characters before the "...see more" fold decide distribution. Best practices converge on 3-5 hashtags, mandatory ALT text (max ~120 chars) and SRT captions for accessibility, 1300-1900 char text posts, vertical/square video, and reposting "with thoughts" (100+ words) rather than plain one-click reshares.

guidelines.guideline: Default to native documents/carousels as the highest-ROI organic format; the confirmed 2026 ER ordering is document/carousel > multi-image > video > image > text.
guidelines.how: Document/carousel ER ~6.6-7.0% (+14% YoY), multi-image ~6.8%, video ~5.6-5.9%, single image ~5.0-5.2%, text lowest. Build a content mix weighted toward carousels and video, reserving plain text for fast opinion/POV posts.
guidelines.why: Carousels and documents produce 2-3x the dwell time of text/image posts, and the 2026 algorithm weights dwell time, saves and comment depth above clicks.

guidelines.guideline: Write 1300-1900 characters (within the 3000 hard limit), front-load a scroll-stopping hook in the first line, and break into 1-2 sentence paragraphs with white space.
guidelines.how: 3000-char limit (spaces, line breaks, hashtags and @mentions all count). The '...see more' fold truncates at ~140-150 chars on mobile and ~200-220 on desktop, so the hook must land before that. Posts of 1300-1900 chars report ~47% higher engagement than shorter ones. One idea per paragraph, generous line breaks, optional bullets.
guidelines.why: The first line decides the click on 'see more'; white space improves read-completion and dwell time, which the algorithm rewards.

guidelines.guideline: Use 1080x1080 (square) or 1080x1350 (4:5 portrait) and ALWAYS add ALT text.
guidelines.how: Feed images support 3:1 to 4:5 aspect ratios; recommended 1080x1080 or 1080x1350; min 552x276; PNG or JPEG; ~5MB practical cap (3MB for Page images). ALT text max ~120 chars, added via the 'Add alt text' option on the image before posting.
guidelines.why: Portrait/square images claim more vertical feed real estate; ALT text is required for screen-reader accessibility and adds SEO-relevant keyword context.

guidelines.guideline: Use multi-image (tiled) posts of ~3-5 images when you have a visual sequence; they are among the highest-engagement formats.
guidelines.how: Up to 20 images per post; multi-image ER ~6.8% in 2026. Keep the first image strong since it sets the thumbnail. Add ALT text to each image.
guidelines.why: Tiled layouts invite taps/expansion, increasing interaction and dwell time.

guidelines.guideline: Publish native PDF carousels of ~8-12 slides at 1080x1080 (1:1) or 1080x1350 (4:5) with a bold cover slide and a clear CTA on the last slide.
guidelines.how: Upload as PDF (also DOC/DOCX/PPT/PPTX, but PDF renders most reliably); max 300 pages; max 100MB; recommended 1080x1080 or 1080x1350; min body font 14pt. Optimal swipe length is 8-12 slides. Cover slide must carry the hook; closing slide drives the action.
guidelines.why: This is the #1 dwell-time format (2-3x text/image) and the highest ER; large fonts and mobile-first ratios keep slides legible on phones.

guidelines.guideline: Post native (not YouTube-linked) video in vertical 9:16 or square 1:1, keep it short, add a strong silent-readable cover and burned-in or SRT captions.
guidelines.how: Aspect ratios 1:2.4 to 2.4:1; optimal 9:16, 4:5, 1:1, 16:9. Duration 3s min; 15 min max desktop, 10 min mobile/Page; sweet spot ~30-90s. Resolution up to 4096x2304 (1080p recommended), 10-60fps, ~5GB max, MP4/H.264. Upload an SRT caption file and a custom cover/thumbnail.
guidelines.why: Video ER ~5.6-5.9%; most feeds autoplay muted, so captions and a legible cover are essential; vertical/square maximizes mobile screen share.

guidelines.guideline: Add SRT captions to every video, ALT text to every image/document, and use camelCase (PascalCase) hashtags.
guidelines.how: Upload .SRT (don't rely solely on auto-captions; review them). ALT text max ~120 chars. Write #SocialMediaMarketing not #socialmediamarketing so screen readers parse each word.
guidelines.why: Accessibility is both an inclusion requirement and a reach signal (captioned video and described images get more completion and engagement).

guidelines.guideline: Use 3-5 relevant, specific hashtags placed at the end of the post.
guidelines.how: Mix 1-2 broad + 2-3 niche tags. They count toward the 3000-char limit. Avoid 10+ tags (spam signal) and avoid stuffing them mid-text.
guidelines.why: 3-5 is the consistently recommended range; more dilutes relevance and can look spammy without improving reach.

guidelines.guideline: Keep outbound links OUT of the post body; if a link is essential, add it in the first comment AND/OR edit it into the post after initial distribution.
guidelines.how: A body link cuts median reach roughly 18-60% (commonly cited ~50%, 'halves reach'). The first-comment workaround is now partly patched (algorithm detects bridge behavior) and only recovers ~10-15%. Best practice: write a fully native post, drop the link in comment one, and tell readers 'link in comments'.
guidelines.why: LinkedIn's priority is keeping users on-platform; any post that funnels attention off-platform is demoted in distribution.

guidelines.guideline: Always 'Repost with your thoughts' (100+ words of original commentary); avoid plain one-click reposts.
guidelines.how: In 2026 a typical original post earns ~28 median likes, a repost-with-thoughts ~8, and a plain one-click repost only ~2. Commentary under ~50 words performs ~3x worse than 100+ words. A repost-with-thoughts is treated as a new post and gets its own distribution.
guidelines.why: Plain reposts get almost no independent reach; substantive added perspective makes it a fresh, rankable post.

guidelines.guideline: Use polls sparingly as quick engagement bait, with a sharp question, 2-4 options and a 1-week duration.
guidelines.how: Poll question max 140 chars; each option max 30 chars; up to 4 options; duration options are 1 day / 3 days / 1 week / 2 weeks (1 week is the usual default). Add a comment explaining context and asking for 'other' answers.
guidelines.why: Polls drive fast votes/comments early, but overuse erodes credibility; the tight char limits force a crisp question.

guidelines.guideline: Use native Articles for evergreen, SEO-indexable long-form (how-tos, thought leadership) rather than feed reach.
guidelines.how: Article body limit ~110,000 characters. Add a 1200x627 cover image, descriptive H2/H3 subheads and keyword-rich title; Articles are Google-indexable unlike posts.
guidelines.why: Articles win search/evergreen discovery and authority but get less immediate feed distribution than native posts; pair with a feed post that teases them.

guidelines.guideline: Run a recurring LinkedIn Newsletter to build an owned subscriber base with built-in push/email/in-app notifications.
guidelines.how: No practical character limit. On publish, all subscribers receive push + in-app + email notifications; new followers get an auto-invite to subscribe. Keep a consistent cadence and title. One newsletter per author/Page is the norm.
guidelines.why: Newsletters convert one-time readers into a notified, returning audience - the closest LinkedIn gets to owned distribution.

guidelines.guideline: Use LinkedIn Live for real-time authority moments (launches, AMAs, panels), 20-60 minutes long, promoted via a paired Event.
guidelines.how: Max stream length 4 hours; recommended >15 min and ideally <=1 hour. Requires a third-party streaming tool (Restream, StreamYard, etc.) and access approval. Promote with a LinkedIn Event and go live at a pre-announced time.
guidelines.why: Live notifies your network in real time and generates high comment volume and dwell; the 20-60 min window balances depth with retention.

guidelines.guideline: Create a LinkedIn Event for every webinar/live/launch to capture registrations and trigger network-wide notifications.
guidelines.how: Events let attendees register, get reminders, and share; pair with a LinkedIn Live or external session. Post updates to the Event feed before and after.
guidelines.why: Events surface to the registrant's network and drive recurring reminder touchpoints, amplifying reach for the linked Live/session.

guidelines.guideline: Contribute selectively to Collaborative Articles for visibility, but note the incentive has weakened in 2026.
guidelines.how: LinkedIn retired the gold Community Top Voice badge for contributors (existing badges expire after a grace window). The feature still exists and supports up to ~5 co-authors with improved editing, but no new badge reward.
guidelines.why: Still a discovery surface, but with the badge gone the strategic upside is lower - prioritize owned formats over chasing contributions.

guidelines.guideline: Reply to every comment within ~30 minutes and seed the conversation in the first hour after posting.
guidelines.how: Responding within 30 min reportedly yields ~64% more comments and ~2.3x more views; the algorithm heavily weights engagement in the first 60 minutes. Ask a question at the end of the post and reply with substantive (not one-word) answers to deepen threads.
guidelines.why: Early comment velocity and back-and-forth depth are top ranking signals; author replies count as fresh engagement and extend the post's active window.

guidelines.guideline: Tag only people/Pages genuinely relevant to the post (ideally those likely to engage), and cap it to a handful.
guidelines.how: @mentions count toward the 3000-char limit. Tag co-authors, quoted sources or featured clients - not a wall of names. Over-tagging uninvolved people is treated as spam and can suppress reach.
guidelines.why: Relevant tags trigger notifications and pull in engaged commenters early; irrelevant mass-tagging is a negative quality signal.

guidelines.guideline: Use emojis sparingly for visual hierarchy and scannability, not decoration.
guidelines.how: Emojis count toward the character limit. Use them as bullet markers or to break sections (1 per section max in B2B contexts). Avoid emoji-heavy openers that read as low-trust.
guidelines.why: A few emojis improve readability and dwell; excess undermines professional credibility and can look like engagement-bait.

summary: In 2026 LinkedIn content strategy is governed by the shift from a Social Graph to an Interest Graph: reach is earned by topical authority, dwell time, saves (≈5x a like) and meaningful comments (≈15x a like), not by raw posting volume. The proven cadence sweet spot is 3-5 posts/week (2-5 acceptable), with sharp diminishing returns and active quality-penalty above 5/week; the algorithm punishes a drop in quality more than it rewards frequency, and posts should be spaced ~20-28h apart. Personal profiles dramatically outperform company pages (commonly cited 8x; more conservative Sprout Q1-2026 medians ≈4.7% vs ~1-2%), so winning strategies are person-led / employee-advocacy driven, with the company page used for proof, jobs and amplification. Content is built on 3-4 stable pillars (held ≥90 days) using mixes like 3:2:1 weekly (Authority:Engagement:Conversion) and mapped to a TOFU/MOFU/BOFU funnel (~50/30/20). Broetry is dead — the algorithm now penalizes low-value single-line formatting and engagement bait, rewarding value-driven storytelling (named frameworks, data, case studies). Best windows are Tue-Thu 10am-2pm with Wednesday the standout day (11am and a rising 4pm slot), plus a growing 3-8pm afternoon/evening window; weekends underperform for B2B. External links in-body cut reach ~18-55% (estimates vary), first-comment links help less than before, and engagement pods are now actively detected and shadowbanned.

guidelines.guideline: Define 3-4 fixed content pillars and commit to them for at least 90 days before changing
guidelines.how: Use a tested triad such as Authority (educational/insight), Proof (case studies, results, social proof) and Personality (POV, behind-the-scenes, personal story); or pillar around the audience's top 3-4 recurring problems. Keep pillars topically coherent to build the Interest-Graph topic authority the 2026 algorithm rewards.
guidelines.why: The 2026 Interest Graph assigns reach based on consistent topical relevance/expertise; scattered topics dilute the authority signal and reduce distribution.

guidelines.guideline: Build the program around individual profiles (founder, execs, SMEs, employees), not the brand page
guidelines.how: Run a person-led / employee-advocacy model: the company page handles culture, jobs, product proof and reshares/amplifies; people carry the reach. Aim most organic reach budget at personal profiles.
guidelines.why: Personal profiles outperform company pages (commonly cited ~8x; conservative Sprout Q1-2026 medians ≈4.7% vs ~1-2% engagement). Page-centric strategies fight the algorithm.

guidelines.guideline: Use a simple weekly ratio such as 3:2:1 — 3 Authority, 2 Engagement, 1 Conversion
guidelines.how: Authority = educational/framework/data posts that build credibility; Engagement = posts engineered for comments/conversation; Conversion = soft-CTA (case study, lead magnet, booking link, DM trigger). Alternative format mix for carousel-heavy creators: ~40% educational, ~40% carousels, ~20% personal-branding.
guidelines.why: Balances credibility, conversation (the highest-weighted signal) and pipeline without over-selling, which suppresses reach.

guidelines.guideline: Map every post to TOFU/MOFU/BOFU and balance roughly 50% TOFU, 30% MOFU, 20% BOFU
guidelines.how: TOFU = problem-aware education, POV, trends, hot takes (broad reach). MOFU = comparison guides, frameworks, webinars, how-to, demos (nurture). BOFU = case studies, customer stories, ROI/results, offers, DM-CTAs (convert). Tag each calendar slot with its stage.
guidelines.why: Keeps a healthy top-of-funnel for reach while still generating qualified pipeline; all-BOFU feeds collapse reach, all-TOFU feeds generate no leads.

guidelines.guideline: Post 3-5 times per week (2-5 acceptable); never sacrifice quality to hit a number
guidelines.how: Pick a sustainable rhythm (e.g. Mon/Wed/Fri or Tue/Wed/Thu). Space posts ~20-28h apart so each clears its initial distribution test before the next competes. Treat >5/week as the zone of diminishing returns.
guidelines.why: 3-5/week is the highest-ROI band; the 2026 algorithm penalizes quality drops, so 3 strong posts beat 7 mediocre ones, and stacking posts cannibalizes reach.

guidelines.guideline: Default to Tue-Thu, 10am-2pm in the audience's time zone, with Wednesday as the priority day
guidelines.how: Wednesday 11am is the single strongest slot; Wednesday ~4pm is the rising standout. Test an afternoon/evening window (3-8pm) which now outperforms early mornings. Video performs best 12-2pm and 5-7pm. Avoid weekends for B2B.
guidelines.why: Mid-week midday is where professional attention concentrates; afternoons/evenings are rising; weekend B2B engagement is low across every dataset.

guidelines.guideline: Engineer the first 1-2 lines (the visible 'see more' preview) as a standalone scroll-stopper
guidelines.how: Keep hooks short (under ~10 words outperform by ~40%); use curiosity gaps, contrarian claims, a specific number/result, or a mid-scene story opening with tension. Write like a real thought, not a headline. Front-load the most surprising element before the 'see more' cut (~140-210 chars on mobile).
guidelines.why: ~65% of users decide to expand based on the opening line alone; the hook drives an estimated 80% of performance and a stronger hook can yield 2-5x engagement. Curiosity/contrarian hooks outperform others ~2.3x.

guidelines.guideline: Structure posts with proven narrative frameworks and name your frameworks
guidelines.how: Use Hook-Story-Lesson, PAS (Problem-Agitate-Solution), Before-After-Bridge, Hero's Journey, listicle, contrarian-take, or behind-the-scenes. Give reusable ideas a memorable name (e.g. 'The 3-C Framework') to trigger saves. Back claims with data, examples or case studies.
guidelines.why: The 2026 algorithm rewards 'Knowledge Value' and saves; named, teachable frameworks are saved/shared because they're reusable thinking tools.

guidelines.guideline: Abandon broetry (one-sentence-per-line drama); write scannable but substantive prose
guidelines.how: Use short paragraphs (1-3 lines) with real content, not artificial line breaks. Lead with value, avoid manufactured suspense and engagement bait ('comment YES below'). Use white space for readability, not theatrics.
guidelines.why: LinkedIn now penalizes low-value formatting and engagement bait; dwell time and meaningful comments — not gimmicks — drive reach.

guidelines.guideline: Keep external links out of the post body; use in-post DM/comment CTAs and put links in the first comment or via 'see more' when needed
guidelines.how: Replace body URLs with a directive CTA (e.g. "DM me 'GUIDE'", "comment WORD and I'll send it"). If a link is essential, place it in the first comment — but expect reduced help. Avoid links in carousel CTA slides; use a keyword/DM trigger instead.
guidelines.why: In-body links cut reach roughly 18-55% (estimates vary). The first-comment workaround now helps less (and link-bearing comments can be suppressed up to ~80%), so on-platform CTAs preserve distribution.

guidelines.guideline: Optimize the first ~60 minutes for genuine comments, not pod activity
guidelines.how: Post when your audience is active, reply fast and substantively to early comments to spark threads, and ask a genuine question that invites real answers. Prioritize comments and saves over likes.
guidelines.why: Early meaningful engagement signals quality and unlocks wider distribution; comments ≈15x and saves ≈5x a like in weight.

guidelines.guideline: Do not use engagement pods or reciprocal-engagement schemes
guidelines.how: Build real conversations: engage authentically on others' posts in your niche, nurture genuine relationships, and let comments come organically. Avoid coordinated like/comment groups and scheduled reciprocal engagement.
guidelines.why: In 2026 LinkedIn actively detects reciprocal/pod patterns and applies silent shadowbans; pod comments are generic, generate no threads, add ~zero algorithmic value and can trigger spam detection.

guidelines.guideline: Repurpose every strong idea across multiple native formats
guidelines.how: Turn a blog/long-form into a document/carousel (sweet spot 8-15 slides), extract one insight into a text post, pull a stat into a graphic, and seed a newsletter section. Keep assets native (uploaded docs/video) rather than off-platform links.
guidelines.why: Native multi-format repurposing maximizes reach per idea; carousels/documents and frameworks are top-performing 2026 formats and keep users on-platform (better dwell time).

guidelines.guideline: Run a structured calendar tagging each slot with pillar, funnel stage and format
guidelines.how: Plan weekly/monthly: assign each post a pillar (1 of 3-4), a funnel stage (TOFU/MOFU/BOFU), a format (text/carousel/video/document/newsletter) and a slot (day/time). Batch-create, then schedule into the Tue-Thu midday windows. Review quarterly before changing pillars.
guidelines.why: A pillar+stage+format matrix guarantees mix balance, prevents over-selling, and sustains the 3-5/week cadence without quality dips.

guidelines.guideline: Use the company page for proof, recruiting and amplification rather than as the primary reach engine
guidelines.how: Post culture, product proof, jobs, milestones and curated employee/leader content; reshare employee posts; run any paid amplification from the page. Keep thought-leadership reach on personal profiles.
guidelines.why: Company pages carry far lower organic engagement (~1-2% vs personal ~4.7%+); their best ROI is credibility, hiring and as an amplification hub.

summary: In 2026 LinkedIn Ads remains the premium B2B paid channel, with high costs (cross-industry CPC ~$5.74, CPM ~$33.80, CPL ~$94, all up ~8-9% YoY) justified by precise professional targeting and high lead intent. The headline shift is the dominance of Thought Leader Ads (TLAs) — organic posts from individual employees/executives sponsored by the brand — which deliver dramatically higher engagement (commonly cited at ~2.68% median CTR vs ~0.42% for single-image ads, with some studies citing 4.65% vs 0.68%; 40-50%+ lower CPL; multiples more comments) and now support both employees and non-employees with author approval/auto-approval workflows. Targeting evolved away from legacy Lookalikes toward Predictive Audiences (AI-built from CRM closed-won seed lists, ~14-21% better CPL) and tighter Matched Audiences/ABM (company lists match 90-98%). Measurement combines the Insight Tag with the server-side Conversions API (CAPI) using shared event_id deduplication and the li_fat_id first-party click ID to offset cookie loss. Lead Gen Forms still convert ~5x landing pages (now normalizing to ~8-13%), and campaign setup flows objective-first (Awareness, Engagement, Lead Gen, Website Visits/Conversions, Video Views, Job Applicants) before format and bidding (Maximum Delivery default, Cost Cap, Manual).

guidelines.guideline: Default to Single Image Ads for scalable feed reach but expect the lowest engagement (~0.42-0.54% CTR, highest CPL ~$104); use them as the volume workhorse, not the engagement driver.
guidelines.how: Sponsored Content > Single image. Use 1200x627 (1.91:1 landscape) or 1200x1200 (1:1 square); vertical 4:5/1:1.91 deliver mobile-only. JPG/PNG/GIF, max 5MB. Intro text <=150 chars (visible before truncation), headline <=70, description <=100.
guidelines.why: It is the cheapest to produce and broadest-reaching but the algorithm and members treat it as an ad, depressing CTR; reserve budget weighting for higher-engagement formats.

guidelines.guideline: Use Carousel Ads (2-10 swipeable cards) for storytelling, multi-feature product walk-throughs, or step sequences; they out-CTR single image (~0.78%).
guidelines.how: Each card 1080x1080 (1:1), JPG/PNG, max 10MB/card. Headline per card <=45 chars; intro text keep <=150 chars (hard max 255). 3-5 cards is the practical sweet spot.
guidelines.why: Interactivity increases dwell time and gives multiple hooks, and the format signals depth rather than a single sales message.

guidelines.guideline: Use Video Ads for awareness and to build retargetable warm audiences; keep awareness videos to 15-30 seconds and front-load the message in the first 3 seconds (sound-off captions mandatory).
guidelines.how: 16:9 (1920x1080), 1:1 (1080x1080) or 4:5 (1080x1350, best mobile feed real estate). MP4 preferred (also MOV/ASF), max 200MB, 3s-30min allowed. Add SRT captions; design for muted autoplay.
guidelines.why: Video earns the highest engagement of any content type, and video-view audiences can be retargeted; but most viewing is silent and mobile, so captions and a fast hook are non-negotiable.

guidelines.guideline: Use Document Ads to gate or preview eBooks/whitepapers/guides directly in-feed — they post the strongest standard-format CTR (~1.10%) and ~3.4x the engagement of static images.
guidelines.how: Upload PDF/DOC/PPT; LinkedIn renders an in-feed preview. Pair with a Lead Gen Form to capture the download. Keep the first preview page as the hook.
guidelines.why: In-feed reading removes click friction and signals genuine value, driving both engagement and lead capture without sending users off-platform.

guidelines.guideline: Use Message/Conversation Ads sparingly for high-value, late-funnel offers (demos, event invites) — they are intrusive, desktop-only, and gated by member messaging preferences, so reserve for tight ABM lists.
guidelines.how: Conversation Ads: up to 5 CTA buttons per message (<=25 chars each, up to 50 total per flow), message text up to 8,000 chars, custom footer up to 20,000 chars, optional banner JPG/PNG 300x250 max 2MB (desktop). Set a clear single next step per branch.
guidelines.why: Open/response rates are high when relevant but deliverability is throttled and over-use damages brand perception; the format only pays off with strong personalization and a concrete offer.

guidelines.guideline: Use Spotlight and Follower Ads (Dynamic Ads, right-rail desktop only) for personalized branding and page-follower growth, not for primary conversion volume.
guidelines.how: Spotlight: headline <=50 chars, description <=70, CTA <=18 chars, company logo 100x100, optional background 300x250. Dynamic ads auto-insert the member's name/photo. Follower Ads drive Page follows.
guidelines.why: Right-column placement and profile personalization lift recognition cheaply, but desktop-only inventory and small size cap their scale and conversion power.

guidelines.guideline: Use Event Ads to promote LinkedIn or third-party events with built-in registration; ensure the destination is a LinkedIn Event page URL.
guidelines.how: URL must be a LinkedIn Event page, http(s):// prefix, max 2000 chars. Format auto-pulls date/time/location with a registration button.
guidelines.why: Native event cards reduce registration friction and surface live, time-relevant details, improving sign-up rates over a generic link.

guidelines.guideline: Make Thought Leader Ads (TLAs) the engagement and top-of-funnel cornerstone of the 2026 plan — they deliver roughly 2.5-7x the CTR of brand ads (cited ~2.68% vs ~0.42% single image; some studies 4.65% vs 0.68%), 40-50%+ lower CPL, lower CPC (~$2.29 vs ~$13.23), and multiples more comments.
guidelines.how: In Campaign Manager use the Brand Awareness or Engagement objective, then select an employee's/executive's organic post as the creative. Run as promoted organic posts, not purpose-built ads; favor raw phone video under ~90s and hooks under ~150 words before the 'see more'.
guidelines.why: Members trust and engage with individual voices far more than logos; the higher relevance score lowers auction costs while the human format earns comments and shares that fuel retargeting pools.

guidelines.guideline: Secure author approval before sponsoring any individual's post, and set up auto-approval for ongoing programs; you do NOT need access to the author's personal ad account.
guidelines.how: In Campaign Manager search the employee, find the target post, and request permission — the author is notified and approves/denies. Authors can enable automatic approval for future requests from a specific advertiser (Settings under sponsor permissions). Page-side you need Super/Content admin or Sponsored Content poster, plus Creative Manager+ on the ad account.
guidelines.why: LinkedIn requires explicit content-owner consent to sponsor a personal post; auto-approval removes per-post friction so always-on TLA programs can scale without bottlenecks.

guidelines.guideline: Sponsor non-employees too (customers, partners, advocates) when their post credibly endorses you — TLAs are not limited to staff.
guidelines.how: Same request/approval flow against the external author's post (e.g., a customer testimonial post). Confirm the author consents to commercial use.
guidelines.why: Third-party advocacy carries more credibility than first-party claims; promoting genuine customer voices boosts persuasion while staying within LinkedIn's consent rules.

guidelines.guideline: Build a sustainable cadence: ghost-write/co-create, have the leader review-approve-post organically first, then sponsor the best performers; expect peak paid performance around weeks 8-12 with decay by ~week 19.
guidelines.how: Let posts gather organic signal, then put spend behind winners. Rotate creators (founder/CEO/senior operators) and refresh creative before the ~week 19 fatigue point.
guidelines.why: Sponsoring already-validated organic posts compounds proven engagement, and planned refresh avoids creative fatigue eroding CTR and inflating CPL.

guidelines.guideline: Choose the objective before the format — LinkedIn optimizes literally for the selected objective, so mismatches waste budget.
guidelines.how: Pick from Awareness (impressions/CPM), Engagement (likes/comments/shares/follows), Video Views, Website Visits (clicks), Lead Generation (native forms), Website Conversions (Insight Tag actions), Job Applicants, and Follower/Brand-page growth.
guidelines.why: The auction and delivery algorithm bid toward the chosen KPI; selecting Lead Gen but measuring reach (or vice versa) misdirects optimization.

guidelines.guideline: Use Engagement campaigns deliberately to build retargetable warm audiences, not for direct leads.
guidelines.how: Run Engagement objective; then create an Engagement Matched Audience of ad/page interactors and retarget them with mid/low-funnel Lead Gen or Conversion campaigns.
guidelines.why: Engagers are a high-intent pool; layering retargeting on top of awareness creates a funnel that converts far cheaper than cold lead-gen.

guidelines.guideline: Prefer Job Function + Seniority over Job Title targeting to capture decision-makers without missing title variants.
guidelines.how: Combine Job Functions (e.g., Marketing, IT) with Job Seniorities (e.g., Director, VP, CXO). Layer Company Size and Industry. Use Skills or LinkedIn Group membership for niche practitioner targeting; consider Company Growth Rate for prioritization.
guidelines.why: Titles are inconsistent and self-reported; function+seniority consolidates many title variations and reliably reaches the buying committee.

guidelines.guideline: Keep audiences in the workable size band and adjust by adding/removing one dimension at a time; avoid sub-50k audiences where delivery becomes unreliable.
guidelines.how: Target ~50,000-200,000 for awareness. If forecast <50k remove a dimension; if >300k add one. LinkedIn's hard minimum to serve is 300 members. Avoid over-layering >2-3 attributes which thins reach and inflates CPM.
guidelines.why: Too-small audiences starve the algorithm of optimization data and spike costs; too-broad wastes spend on irrelevant impressions.

guidelines.guideline: Always configure exclusions — one of the highest-ROI, most under-used levers.
guidelines.how: Exclude own employees (company name), competitors, junior/non-purchasing seniorities, irrelevant industries/company sizes, and already-converted accounts/contacts (CRM exclusion list). Turn OFF or tightly monitor Audience Expansion and the LinkedIn Audience Network for precision campaigns.
guidelines.why: Exclusions prevent paying premium CPCs to reach people who can't or already did buy, directly lowering CPL and improving lead quality.

guidelines.guideline: Run ABM via Company List Matched Audiences for a 50-500 account target list — it converts ~2.7x better than broad targeting.
guidelines.how: Upload company name + domain for 90-98% match rates. For contacts, upload work emails (70-85% match; personal emails only 40-60%). Layer seniority/function on the company list to hit the buying committee. Use Website Visits objective on ABM to capture per-account click data.
guidelines.why: Concentrating spend on named in-market accounts aligns paid media with sales priorities and yields materially higher conversion than attribute-only targeting.

guidelines.guideline: Replace legacy Lookalikes with Predictive Audiences seeded from CRM closed-won/converter lists — LinkedIn deprecated traditional Lookalike creation in the 2024-2025 transition.
guidelines.how: Build a Predictive Audience from a clean seed list of past converters; LinkedIn's ML expands using graph + engagement signals. Benchmarks show ~14-21% lower CPL and ~19% CTR lift vs broad targeting.
guidelines.why: AI seeded on actual revenue outcomes finds statistically similar likely-converters, scaling reach without sacrificing the conversion quality of a hand-built list.

guidelines.guideline: Use native Lead Gen Forms over landing pages for in-platform capture — they convert roughly 5x better (LGF ~8-13% vs LP ~1.6-2.35%).
guidelines.how: Attach an LGF to Lead Gen objective campaigns. Use pre-filled profile fields (name, email, job title, company, etc.). Keep to 3-4 fields; every removed field lifts conversion ~10-20%, and each free-text custom question costs ~3-4% submission rate.
guidelines.why: Pre-population from the member's profile removes nearly all friction, so shorter forms maximize volume while a single qualifying question protects quality.

guidelines.guideline: Add at most one or two custom questions to pre-qualify (budget/timeline/authority), and connect forms to CRM with sub-24h follow-up.
guidelines.how: Up to 3 custom questions allowed (multiple-choice or single-line text), within a 12-field/question form max. Sync leads to CRM/marketing automation; route to sales with SLA <24h. Include a thank-you message and offer link.
guidelines.why: A qualifying question filters low-intent leads cheaply, and fast follow-up on a high-intent signal sharply increases the odds of a real conversation.

guidelines.guideline: Install the Insight Tag on all pages and pair it with the Conversions API (CAPI) for resilient, deduplicated tracking in a cookie-restricted 2026 environment.
guidelines.how: Add the Insight Tag site-wide (Campaign Manager > Measurement > Insights/Manage Sources). Enable Enhanced Conversions. Implement CAPI directly or via a LinkedIn Marketing Partner/GTM; in Campaign Manager > Measurement generate the access token. Send a shared event_id on both browser and server events (LinkedIn keeps the browser event, discards the duplicate server one). Capture li_fat_id from landing URLs/cookies and pass it as LINKEDIN_FIRST_PARTY_ADS_TRACKING_UUID. A 201 response confirms acceptance.
guidelines.why: Browser-only tracking misses conversions from ad blockers and privacy restrictions; server-side CAPI recovers them, and shared-ID dedup prevents double-counting while li_fat_id restores first-party attribution.

guidelines.guideline: Start campaigns on Maximum Delivery to gather data, then move proven campaigns to Manual bidding for cost control; treat Cost Cap as a last resort.
guidelines.how: Maximum Delivery (automated, default) spends the full budget for max results. Manual sets an exact max bid per auction for full control. Cost Cap targets an average cost per result but is least-used and not insider-recommended. Minimum $10/day allowed; budget $50-100/day per campaign and ~$5,000-10,000/month total for a meaningful test.
guidelines.why: Max Delivery accelerates learning, Manual reclaims efficiency once you know your true cost per result, and under-funded tests never gather enough signal to optimize.

guidelines.guideline: Run structured A/B tests changing one variable at a time and measure against the objective's true KPI, not vanity metrics.
guidelines.how: Duplicate a campaign and vary a single element (creative, headline, audience, or format). Hold spend/duration constant. Compare CTR/CVR/CPL per the objective. Refresh creative before fatigue (TLAs ~week 19).
guidelines.why: Isolating variables yields reliable causal learnings, and aligning the success metric to the objective prevents optimizing for the wrong outcome.

guidelines.guideline: Control where ads appear via the LinkedIn Audience Network (LAN) settings and apply brand-safety/exclusion lists for off-platform placements.
guidelines.how: Toggle LAN on/off per campaign; if on, apply publisher block lists and category exclusions. Keep precision/ABM campaigns on-platform only. Monitor placement reports.
guidelines.why: LAN extends reach beyond LinkedIn but onto third-party inventory of variable quality; controlling it protects brand context and CPL efficiency.

guidelines.guideline: Budget for seniority and industry premiums — targeting executives and regulated industries multiplies costs.
guidelines.how: Plan for C-Suite CPC ~$14.85, VP/SVP ~$11.20 vs individual contributor ~$3.18. Industry CPC ranges ~$3.12 (Nonprofit) to ~$7.95 (Legal); CPL ~$48 (Nonprofit) to ~$148 (Financial Services). Reach senior buyers via cheaper function+seniority or TLAs where possible.
guidelines.why: Auction prices scale with how contested and valuable the audience is; knowing the premium prevents underbudgeting and informs format choice.

pitfalls: Defaulting to Maximum Delivery permanently — it spends the full budget for LinkedIn's benefit; move proven campaigns to Manual bidding for cost control.
Leaving Audience Expansion or the LinkedIn Audience Network on for precision/ABM campaigns, silently widening reach to off-target or low-quality off-platform inventory and inflating CPL.
Over-layering targeting (4+ attributes) which shrinks audiences below the 50k workable band, starving optimization and spiking CPM/CPC.
Building Lead Gen Forms with too many fields or multiple free-text questions — each free-text question can cut submissions 3-4% and every extra field costs 10-20% CVR.
Relying on the Insight Tag alone in 2026 — ad blockers and privacy restrictions lose conversions; not deploying CAPI with shared event_id dedup leaves attribution incomplete.
Forgetting exclusions (own employees, competitors, junior roles, converted accounts), paying premium CPCs to reach non-buyers.

Trying to run Thought Leader Ads without author approval/auto-approval, or assuming you need access to the author's personal ad account — you only need their content consent plus Page/ad-account roles.
Choosing a campaign objective that doesn't match the real KPI (e.g., Awareness when you need leads); LinkedIn optimizes literally for the selected objective.
Using legacy Lookalike Audiences — traditional Lookalike creation was deprecated in 2024-2025; use Predictive Audiences instead.
Letting Thought Leader / evergreen creatives run past fatigue (~week 19 for TLAs) without refresh, eroding CTR and raising CPL.
Treating Conversation/Message Ads as a broad-reach format — they are desktop-only, gated by member preferences, and brand-damaging if over-used; reserve for tight, high-value lists.
Not following up Lead Gen Form submissions within 24 hours, wasting a high-intent signal.
Sending vertical single-image creatives expecting desktop delivery — 4:5 and 1:1.91 serve mobile-only.
Underfunding tests below ~$5,000/month, producing too little data to optimize reliably.
