pitfalls: Using ANY browser-extension or bot to automate connections, messages, or scraping - this directly violates the Prohibited Software policy and is the #1 cause of the ~2M monthly restrictions; Chrome-injection tools are especially detectable via fingerprinting.
Joining or running engagement pods or pre-arranged like/comment groups - detected at ~97% accuracy and punished with a silent shadowban (no warning), not a removable post.
Engagement-bait wording ('Comment YES', 'tag 3 friends', follow-for-follow) - ~60% algorithmic reach penalty in 2026.
Blasting identical/templated messages or pitching immediately after a connection is accepted - top spam-filter trigger even within volume limits, because LinkedIn scores message quality.
Letting pending invitations pile up past 2,500 or sending invites with a low acceptance rate - tightens your weekly cap and risks 'invitation limit reached' restrictions.
Posting unlabeled AI-generated images/text/voice - breaches disclosure norms now and the EU AI Act from Aug 2, 2026.
Fake or embellished profile details (invented title, borrowed photo, fabricated credentials) - a warning-skip impersonation trigger that can permanently restrict the account.
Logging in from many IPs/locations/devices rapidly (VPN-hopping, shared accounts) - triggers security restrictions even for legitimate travelers.
Creating a new account after a permanent ban - device-fingerprint and IP tracking re-restrict it almost immediately.

Cold B2B outreach in the EU/UK with no documented Legitimate Interest Assessment, no opt-out, or a commercial pitch in the first connection note - GDPR/ePrivacy exposure on top of LinkedIn spam rules.
Running prohibited ad categories (political, MLM, gambling/sweepstakes, weapons, adult, misleading health claims) or discriminatory targeting - ads removed and advertiser account restricted.
Skipping alt text and captions, scattering lowercase hashtags mid-text, or stacking emojis as list markers - fails accessibility expectations and degrades screen-reader experience.
Using unauthorized logos, trademarks, or others' copyrighted images - exposes you to DMCA takedowns and repeat-infringer account action.

checklists.name: DO - Compliance essentials
items: Use your real name, real photo, and accurate credentials on personal profiles; create Company Pages only as an authorized representative.
Keep all activity within limits: ~100-200 invites/week, <2,500 pending invites, <30,000 connections, ~100-150 messages/week.
Personalize every connection request and message; lead with relevance, keep the first message commercial-offer-free.
Withdraw pending invitations older than ~30 days and maintain a >30-40% acceptance rate.
Disclose AI-generated/edited content within the first two sentences or via LinkedIn's built-in AI indicator.
Add descriptive alt text to every image and accurate captions/SRT to every video.
Write hashtags in CamelCase, 3-5 per post, placed at the end; use plain language and intentional emojis.
Document a Legitimate Interest Assessment and provide an easy opt-out for EU/UK B2B outreach.
Use only LinkedIn's official APIs/partner programs for any automation, and act manually otherwise.
Post original, expertise-driven content and engage authentically.
Review Settings & Privacy > Data Privacy to set AI-training and data-use preferences.
Comply with LinkedIn Ads policies and ensure landing pages match ad claims.

checklists.name: DONT - Hard violations
items: Don't use crawlers, bots, scripts, or browser extensions to scrape, copy data, or automate connections/messages.
Don't join or operate engagement pods or pre-arrange likes/reshares.
Don't use engagement-bait ('Comment YES', 'tag 3', follow-for-follow) or lead posts with bare external links.
Don't send identical mass messages or pitch immediately after connecting.
Don't fake your identity, title, credentials, or use someone else's/AI-invented photo; don't impersonate or share/sell accounts.
Don't post hate speech, harassment, sexual advances, threats, violence, or graphic/shocking content.
Don't run scams, MLM, pyramid schemes, phishing, or distribute malware.
Don't post unlabeled AI/synthetic media or undisclosed paid endorsements.
Don't exceed invitation/message caps or let pending invites pile up.
Don't use unauthorized logos, trademarks, or copyrighted material.
Don't run prohibited ads (political, gambling/sweepstakes, weapons, adult, counterfeit, MLM, misleading health claims) or use discriminatory targeting.
Don't create a new account to evade a permanent ban.

summary: Between 2024 and 2026 LinkedIn rebuilt its core mechanics. Creator Mode was retired (Feb-Mar 2024) and its tools (Follow CTA, newsletters, analytics, Featured) were democratized to all profiles; profile hashtags were removed. Thought Leader Ads expanded (Apr 1 2024) to let brands sponsor ANY member's content (not just employees), plus articles/newsletters. A dedicated short-form vertical video feed/tab launched in 2024 (TikTok-style, +36% video viewership). The single biggest shift is the AI ranking migration: 360Brew, a 150B-parameter decoder-only foundation model (arXiv 2501.16450, Jan 27 2025), replaced fragmented ranking systems and rolled into the feed through 2025-early 2026, moving LinkedIn from a Relationship Graph to an Interest Graph (only ~31% of the average feed now comes from 1st-degree connections). Richard van der Blom's Algorithm Insights 2026 (1.3M posts) documents a hard reach reset: views ~-50%, engagement ~-25%, follower growth ~-59% YoY, with reach down ~60% for active creators over two years. The algorithm now rewards dwell time, topic authority, comments and meaningful engagement over likes/hashtags, and actively penalizes external links (~18.8% median reach hit for one in-body link per van der Blom; the first-comment workaround is also now penalized) and unedited generic AI output. Document/PDF carousels (~6.6%) and native vertical video lead engagement; text-only lags (~2%). Skills-based hiring accelerated with AI skill verification, and the creator economy matured (450k+ verified creators, monetization programs).

guidelines.guideline: Stop referencing 'Creator Mode' as a toggle to turn on; it was retired Feb-Mar 2024
guidelines.how: Its features (Follow as primary CTA, Newsletters, creator analytics, Featured section, About-at-top) are now default for every profile. Set Follow vs Connect under profile edit; build the Featured section and a Newsletter directly. Do not advise users to 'enable Creator Mode' — the setting no longer exists.
guidelines.why: Outdated advice signals the strategy is stale; the capabilities still exist but are accessed differently.

guidelines.guideline: Do not add the old 'hashtags I talk about' to profiles — that profile feature was removed Feb 2024
guidelines.how: Topic authority is now inferred semantically by 360Brew from the actual content you post, not from declared profile hashtags. Build authority by posting consistently on a tight topic cluster.
guidelines.why: 360Brew reads natural language; consistent on-topic posting is what now signals expertise.

guidelines.guideline: Optimize for the Interest Graph, not your existing network — strangers see your content based on topical relevance
guidelines.how: Pick a narrow, consistent topic so 360Brew can classify you cleanly (semantic clarity). Roughly 31% of the average feed is now from 1st-degree connections, ~25% from 2nd/3rd degree, ~10% pure Suggested/stranger content. Reach beyond your network depends on topic consistency + engagement quality.
guidelines.why: LinkedIn moved from Relationship Graph (people you know) to Interest Graph (what you engage with), so niche relevance beats follower count.

guidelines.guideline: Write for an LLM-based ranker that reads meaning, not for keyword/hashtag matching
guidelines.how: 360Brew is a 150B-parameter decoder-only foundation model (arXiv 2501.16450) that ingests ~2-3 months of user activity as in-context examples and reasons semantically across 30+ tasks (feed, jobs, PYMK). Use clear, specific, on-topic language; avoid keyword stuffing and hashtag spam.
guidelines.why: Semantic models reward genuine relevance and penalize gaming signals; old metadata tricks no longer move ranking.

guidelines.guideline: Engineer for dwell time (read time), which now outranks reactions
guidelines.how: Use strong 1-2 line hooks above the 'see more' fold, line breaks, and longer-form value so people stop and read. A post read 30 seconds beats 50 quick likes. Avoid 'click bounces' (immediate exits), which get the post deprioritized.
guidelines.why: 360Brew measures time-spent engaging as a top relevance signal; bounce behavior is read as low quality.

guidelines.guideline: Treat the first 60 minutes as decisive for distribution
guidelines.how: LinkedIn first tests a post on ~2-5% of your network; only ~5% of posts that underperform in hour one recover. Post when your audience is active, reply to every early comment, and prompt discussion to win the test.
guidelines.why: Early engagement velocity and dwell determine whether the post is expanded to wider audiences.

guidelines.guideline: Keep links out of the post body; even the first-comment workaround is now penalized
guidelines.how: Per van der Blom 2026, one in-body external link cuts median reach ~18.8% (some practitioner data cites up to ~60% for link-heavy posts). Put the value natively in the post and add the link only after engagement lands, or via DM/'comment for link', knowing the comment trick is now also dampened.
guidelines.why: LinkedIn suppresses off-platform links to keep users in-feed; native content is favored.

guidelines.guideline: Aim for 2-4 high-quality posts per week on personal profiles; do not post daily by default
guidelines.how: Moving from 1 to 2-4 posts/week adds ~1,234 impressions per post (van der Blom). Daily posting shows ~-26% average reach per post and ~-45% reach over time from content fatigue. Company Pages: 3-5 posts/week.
guidelines.why: Quality and topic consistency beat volume under the Interest Graph; oversaturation cannibalizes your own reach.

guidelines.guideline: Prioritize PDF document carousels — the highest-engagement format
guidelines.how: ~6.6% engagement. Use 5-10 slides (engagement drops after slide 10), 1080x1080 (square) or 1080x1350 (portrait), export as PDF for cross-device consistency, strong cover slide hook.
guidelines.why: Documents maximize dwell time (swiping) which 360Brew rewards.

guidelines.guideline: Upload native vertical video (9:16) 30-90 seconds to ride the dedicated video feed
guidelines.how: ~5.6% engagement; native video gets a distribution boost via the video tab launched in 2024 (+36% platform video viewership). Add captions (≈91% watch muted), 1:1 or 9:16, native upload (never YouTube/external embeds).
guidelines.why: LinkedIn surfaces native vertical video in a separate TikTok-style feed plus the main feed — double distribution.

guidelines.guideline: Use the engagement hierarchy: documents (~6.6%) > native video (~5.6%) > image+text (~3.2%) > text-only (~2.0%)
guidelines.how: Default to documents and video for reach-critical posts; reserve text-only for fast takes and conversation starters where comment velocity matters.
guidelines.why: Format choice now materially changes baseline reach under 360Brew.

guidelines.guideline: Design posts to earn comments and replies, not likes
guidelines.how: End with a specific question, reply to commenters with substance (which itself counts as engagement), and seed discussion. Comments and dwell weigh far more than reactions.
guidelines.why: Meaningful conversation is a primary 360Brew quality signal; likes are low-value.

guidelines.guideline: Use 0-3 relevant hashtags max; never spam
guidelines.how: Posts without hashtags can outperform hashtagged ones by 5-10%; 10+ hashtags risk a 30-50% visibility penalty. If used, keep to 3-5 highly specific tags. Hashtags are now a minor/weak signal vs semantic topic classification.
guidelines.why: 360Brew reads topic from content; excessive hashtags look spammy and trigger suppression.

guidelines.guideline: Never publish unedited generic AI output — it gets flagged and throttled
guidelines.how: LinkedIn's NLP classifiers flag generic openers, bullet-heavy structures with no personal voice, and templated frameworks repeated across accounts; flagged low-effort AI posts get sharply reduced initial distribution. Four specific over-used templated phrases each pull ~4-7% below normal reach in 2026. Use AI to draft, then add personal voice, specifics, and lived experience.
guidelines.why: The platform actively de-ranks low-effort AI to fight feed saturation; human signal is the differentiator.

guidelines.guideline: Build a LinkedIn Newsletter to bypass feed-algorithm risk
guidelines.how: Newsletter editions are pushed to subscribers via notification + email regardless of feed ranking. Convert engaged followers to subscribers; publish on a consistent cadence. Available to all profiles since Creator Mode democratization.
guidelines.why: Newsletters give algorithm-independent reach — a hedge against the reach reset.

guidelines.guideline: For company pages, use Thought Leader Ads to sponsor employee AND non-employee member content
guidelines.how: Since Apr 1 2024 brands can sponsor any member's post (with consent), including customers/partners, plus articles and newsletters. Find by name (1st/2nd degree) or post URL in Campaign Manager; ad ties to the parent Page. ~73% of decision-makers trust thought-leadership over marketing materials.
guidelines.why: Member-voiced sponsored content outperforms brand-voiced ads on trust and engagement.

guidelines.guideline: Lead profiles and Pages with demonstrated skills, not just credentials
guidelines.how: Add and get verified on the 2026 'Skills on the Rise' (AI implementation, prompt engineering, plus human skills: executive communication, leadership influence, cross-functional collaboration). Use AI Skill Verification. 93% of recruiters plan to ramp AI use in 2026; 60% say AI surfaces 'hidden gem' talent.
guidelines.why: Employers and AI matching weight demonstrated, verified skills over academic credentials.

guidelines.guideline: Treat LinkedIn as a maturing creator/monetization platform
guidelines.how: 450k+ verified creators globally; monetization via partnerships, consulting, speaking, BrandLink/wave programs; top creators earn $15k-$250k+. Pursue verification, collaborative posts (rolling out from Cannes 2025), and co-hosted Lives to tap others' networks.
guidelines.why: Monetization and collaboration features reward consistent, authentic creators and expand cross-network reach.

guidelines.guideline: Lean into LinkedIn's B2B dominance with intent data + thought leadership
guidelines.how: LinkedIn drives ~80% of B2B social leads. Combine personal-brand thought leadership (which outperforms Page content), employee advocacy, and Thought Leader Ads. Note YMYL/regulated sectors get extra AI-Overview scrutiny off-platform too.
guidelines.why: B2B buyers trust individual expert voices and peer testimonials over corporate Pages.

guidelines.guideline: Build measurable Topic Authority through consistency
guidelines.how: Three inputs: topic consistency across posts, engagement quality, and semantic clarity (clean classification). Avoid topic-hopping; pick a lane for 60-90 days so 360Brew can classify and reward you.
guidelines.why: Topic authority is now an explicit ranking input that expands stranger reach.

guidelines.guideline: Avoid heavy edits right after posting and obvious copy-paste reposting
guidelines.how: Don't edit a post during the golden hour (can reset/dampen the test); vary and personalize repurposed content rather than reposting identical text across accounts (which the AI classifier flags as templated).
guidelines.why: Edits during testing and templated duplication both disrupt or suppress distribution.

guidelines.guideline: Use Pages for consistency and amplification, expecting lower organic reach than people
guidelines.how: 3-5 posts/week, employee advocacy to extend reach, native video and documents, and paid amplification (Thought Leader Ads, Sponsored Content). Personal profiles consistently outreach Pages organically.
guidelines.why: 360Brew's Interest Graph favors individual voices; Pages need advocacy + paid to compete.

pitfalls: Telling users to 'turn on Creator Mode' — it was retired in 2024; the features are now default, accessed differently.
Adding 'hashtags I talk about' to profiles — removed Feb 2024 and irrelevant to 360Brew's semantic ranking.
Assuming hashtags drive reach — they're now a weak signal; 10+ triggers a 30-50% penalty and no-hashtag posts often win.
Putting external links in the post body or relying on the first-comment link trick — both are now penalized.
Posting daily 'for the algorithm' — daily cadence reduces per-post reach ~26% and ~45% over time vs 2-4/week.
Publishing unedited generic AI output — flagged by NLP classifiers and throttled; templated phrases cost 4-7% reach each.
Optimizing for likes — comments and dwell time matter far more under 360Brew.
Editing posts during the golden hour — can reset/dampen distribution testing.
Believing reach drops are a personal failure — there was a platform-wide structural reset (~-50% views); benchmark against the new baseline.
Treating Company Pages as the primary organic engine — personal profiles outreach Pages; Pages need employee advocacy + paid.
Topic-hopping — fragments semantic classification and kills the topic-authority signal.
Embedding YouTube/external video instead of native uploads — loses the vertical-video-feed distribution boost.
Myth that 'dwell time can be faked with long posts' — click-bounce detection deprioritizes posts people open and immediately leave.

checklists.name: 2024->2026 change audit (what to update in any old LinkedIn advice)
items: Remove all 'enable Creator Mode' instructions (retired 2024)
Remove 'add hashtags to your profile' (removed 2024)
Reframe reach expectations to the post-reset baseline (~-50% views)
Replace 'hashtags drive reach' with 'topic consistency drives reach' (360Brew/Interest Graph)
Move links out of post body and drop first-comment link trick
Switch default cadence from daily to 2-4 posts/week
Add AI-editing step so no raw/generic AI text ships
Add dwell-time hook + format upgrade (documents/native video)

checklists.name: Format & distribution checklist per post
items: Pick highest-fit high-engagement format (document > native video > image+text > text)
Hook in first 1-2 lines above the fold
No external link in body
0-3 specific hashtags max
Post Tue-Thu 8-10 AM audience time
Reply to every comment in the first 60 minutes
No edits during golden hour
On-topic for your authority lane
